name: bovine-process

metadata.name: bovine-process
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Processing of Side Effects of ActivityPub Activities for an ActivityPub Server

documentation:
<!--
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2023 Helge

SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
-->

# bovine_process

`bovine_process` consists of the side effect logic of Activity objects. This means it contains the code, the logic that for an incoming object, one executes:

- Store object in bovine_store
- Add reference to inbox
- Perform side effects
- Enque object for bovine_pubsub

And a similar list of effects for outgoing objects, i.e

- Store object in bovine_store
- Add reference to outbox
- Perform side effects
- Send objects to follower's inbox
- Enque object for bovine_pubsub

The behavior defined in this package corresponds to [6. Client to Server Interactions](https://www.w3.org/TR/activitypub/#client-to-server-interactions) and [7. Server to Server Interactions](https://www.w3.org/TR/activitypub/#server-to-server-interactions) of the ActivityPub specification. However, only a small subset of side effects is implemented.

## Implemented Side Effects

- Create, Update, Delete on objects, i.e. basic crud
- Like, Dislike, EmojiReact -> add to likes collection; Undo removes
- Announce -> add to share collection; Undo removes
- The same person can Like, Announce, etc.. multiple times
- Create with inReplyTo -> add to replies collection; Delete removes

- Follow and Accept
  - Outgoing Accept of Follow adds to followers
  - Incoming Accept of Follows adds to following

- [ ] Specify Update checks
- [ ] Authority checks.
- [ ] Refactor for easier customization / extension. Adding a new side effect currently requires publishing a new package. This should not be the case.

## Tests

The folder `tests/data` contains test cases what side effects happen in the database for certain cases.

functions.name: bovine_propan.enqueuers.enqueue_to_inbox
documentation: Enqueues a message for inbox processing

functions.name: bovine_propan.enqueuers.enqueue_to_outbox
documentation: Enqueues a message for outbox processing

variables.name: bovine_propan.enqueuers.enqueue_broker
documentation:
Broker to be used for enqueuing items. amqp uri is determined from BOVINE_AMQP
environment variable. Otherwise `amqp://localhost`

functions.name: bovine_propan.actor.bovine_actor
documentation:
Yields the BovineStoreActor corresponding to the `bovine_name`.
Usage:

```python
async with bovine_actor(bovine_name, session) as actor:
    ...
```

variables.name: bovine_propan.exchanges.processing
documentation:
Exchange used for messages being processed.

Relevant routing_key are:

* `inbox`
* `outbox`

variables.name: bovine_propan.exchanges.processed
documentation: Exchange used for messages having been processed, currently used to feed the EventSources

variables.name: bovine_propan.app
documentation:
faststream app can be run using 

```bash
faststream run bovine_propan/__init__:app
```

classes.name: bovine_propan.types.ProcessingMessage
documentation:
Message being processed

see source for field details

classes.name: bovine_propan.types.SendMessage
documentation:
Message being send

see source for field details

functions.name: bovine_propan.broker.inbox_handler
documentation: Handles events coming to the inbox

functions.name: bovine_propan.broker.inbox_to_event
documentation: Creates a server send event for inbox events

functions.name: bovine_propan.broker.to_send_handler
documentation: Retrieves inbox for recipient and sends data

functions.name: bovine_propan.broker.fetch_object
documentation: Meant for RPC calls. Fetches object and returns it

variables.name: bovine_process.incoming.crud_handlers
documentation: The handlers being called for CRUD operations

variables.name: bovine_process.incoming.social_handlers
documentation: The handlers being called for social interactions, i.e. updating the social graph

variables.name: bovine_process.incoming.default_inbox_process
documentation: Represents the default process undertaken by an inbox item

variables.name: bovine_process.common.interaction_processor
documentation: Defines the processor that handles interactions with an object

functions.name: bovine_process.outgoing.update_id.update_id
documentation:
Assigns a new id to the object in data and possibly to the
object in data["object"]

variables.name: bovine_process.outgoing.default_outbox_process
documentation: Defines the synchronous part of sending an outgoing object

variables.name: bovine_process.outgoing.social_processor
documentation: Processes outgoing social events, i.e. updates the social graph

variables.name: bovine_process.outgoing.default_async_outbox_process
documentation: Defines the asynchronous part of handling an outgoing object

functions.name: bovine_process.common.interactions.like_handler
documentation:
Adds object to the likes collection of the liked object, if

* object being liked is owner by the receiving actor

functions.name: bovine_process.common.interactions.announce_handler
documentation:
Adds object to the shares collection of the announced object, if

* object being announced is owner by the receiving actor

functions.name: bovine_process.common.interactions.reply_handler
documentation:
Adds object to the replies collection. Object being replied to
is determined from `inReplyTo`. Reply is added if the object
belongs to the receiving actor.

functions.name: bovine_process.common.interactions.delete_reply_handler
documentation: If a reply is deleted, removes it from the replies collection

functions.name: bovine_process.common.interactions.undo_handler
documentation:
For an Undo of a Like, Dislike, Announce , they are removed from
the appropriate collection.

variables.name: bovine_process.common.interactions.interaction_handlers
documentation: The handlers being called for interactions

classes.name: bovine_process.common.interactions.InteractionActor

methods.name: bovine_process.common.interactions.InteractionActor.retrieve
documentation:
Retrieves an object

```
param object_id

id of the object

param only_own

if true requires object to be by the actor
```

methods.name: bovine_process.common.interactions.InteractionActor.remove_references
documentation:
Removes references to the object. Used in case an object is
deleted and it is unknown which object it replies to

methods.name: bovine_process.common.interactions.InteractionActor.add_to_interaction
documentation:
Adds to interaction

Example object for `interaction_type = "likes"`

json
{

> "type": "Like",
> "id": interaction,
> "object": object_id

# }

```
param interaction_type

One of , 

param object_id

id of the object being interacted with

param interaction

The id of the interacting object
```

methods.name: bovine_process.common.interactions.InteractionActor.remove_from_interaction
documentation:
Removes from interaction

```
param interaction_type

One of , 

param object_id

id of the object being interacted with

param interaction

The id of the interacting object
```
Abstract class to illustrate what is needed to process interactions

functions.name: bovine_process.process_inbox_item
documentation: Asynchronous processing of an item arriving at the inbox

functions.name: bovine_process.handle_outbox_item
documentation:
Synchronous part of processing an outbox item. In particular
it contains the logic to assign ids to the item

functions.name: bovine_process.process_outbox_item
documentation:
Asynchronous part of processing an outbox item. In particular
it contains the logic of sending the message to all followers

classes.name: bovine_process.types.ProcessingItem
documentation:
Represents an entity being processed

```
param submitter

actor_id of the person submitting the item

param data

item being processed

param meta

used to store data between processing steps
```

functions.name: bovine_process.send_item.send_outbox_item
documentation:
Sends the item to all the specfied outboxes

Collections are resolved if they are part of actor.endpoints and then
resolved via actor.resolve_endpoints.

inboxes from actors are fetched from the database or refetched

classes.name: bovine_process.utils.ByActivityType
documentation:
Simplifies handling activity by type, usage:

```python
by_type = ByActivityType(One=do_one, Two=do_two})

by_type({"type": one}, arg1, arg2) # calls do_one({"type": one}, arg1, arg2)
```

classes.name: bovine_process.utils.ProcessorList
documentation:
Creates a list of processors, usage:

```python
processors = ProcessorList(my_processor,
    ByActivityType(Follow=my_follow_processor))
await processors(item, args)
```
